Easily a contender of blanc des blancs from any French producer. Minuscule hill in Veneto, it was a lovely visit to their cellar built out of WWI tunnels.  White flowers (jasmine), crushed stones, a faint lychee on the nose. No oaking thank God. White peaches, elegant minerality, banana, melon, young apricots, very light citrusy acidity, subtle bitterness, definitely dry but with a sweet note. Yes a bit of brioche.  Would choose this dude any day against non-pinot noir-dominant champagne

Hill Farmers Blues (Mark Knopfler) One of the best Prosecco’s I ever had! It’s got the pedigree: it’s a single vineyard, multi-award winning sparkling. But I didn’t know that when I tasted it: it’s rich on the nose, fruity and sweet, with some lemon jumping out of the acidity. Very good, Cartizze (now a synonym for Dry but it’s also the name of a hill that is in fact a 1.000-foot-high vineyard of 107 hectares of vines, owned by 140 growers, yes I did my homework).

De cor palha medio, aromas de pera e maçã amarela, algum melão fresco, massa de torta, limão e pedra calcaria. Acidez alta, mineralidade m/a, corpo medio perlage persistente, final m/p deixa discreto floral. Complexo, boa estrutura, delicioso!

Al di là dello sfondo per cui ringrazio mia moglie che oggi si è superata in termini di sapore, qualità e guarnizione... Ti spunta questa bella bottiglia che ho ricevuto in regalo qualche annetto fa: delicato e dal perlage finissimo... Ottimo da riprovare!
BB & B Grand Tasting Aromas of citrus and stone fruit. Smooth with nice acidity. Hint of sweetness. Lean and fresh. Decent finish.

Top quality sparkling wine ( Prosecco ) from Italy I would say dominate pear apple peach green apple lemon Aromas of wild flowers and citrus fruits are followed by flavors of apple, minerals white peachapricot, lemon, lime, nectarine and white peach, as well as interesting mineral notes. expect additional brioche and buttery flavors.

Extremely light color and subtle palate at first. Hints of pear and white currant. Some herbs and flavours are developing in time. Finish is very refreshing and crispy. Probably the best Prosecco tasted so far.

Great and opulent nose of stone fruit, peach and pear, pretty well balanced and elegant.  Perfect balance on the palate. The fruit is opulent but not overwhelming, the length lasts, the finish with a touch of salt is good too.

Very pale lemon color with floral and peachy aromas. It’s a creamy, delicate and pleasant prosecco that feel like off-dry, nice structure. The finish could be longer and seems to have a bit too much sugar, but still very good.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

Taste..intense,depth,sweetish,great minerality tone with mild acidity Jkyeo..92 21 1.2026

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

M+ nose/mouth of peach, pear, lime, passionfruit, herb, white flower,  Extra dry(17g/L), Light body, M+ acidity, Low alc(11%), M+ flavor, M finish, M complexity. Drink now.

Gold Medal Winner in the Light & Fruity Category at The Glass of Bubbly Awards 2021

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

Во вкусе яблоко, груша, цитрус, мёд, минералы и горчинка цедры в послевкусии. Отличное содержимое первого бокала в наступившем году!   Prosecco Treviso from the Venetto region.  The color is slightly yellowish, the pearlage is large, the taste is rich.  The taste has apple, pear, citrus, honey, minerals and a bitterness of peel in the aftertaste. Excellent contents of the first glass of the new year!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

Perfectly balanced acidity helps bring across flavours of exotic fruit to the palate to add to above mentioned aromas. Moderate sugar level, much lower than is usually the norm. Really refreshing and very pleasant. Probably the best frizzante I have come across apart from Valdobbiadene stuff. Happy days!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

Vibrant, fresh, tasty))  Very pale straw yellow color. Elegant & stable perlage. Flavors of peach, lemon & lime, green apple & melon. Harmonious fruity finish accenting citrus mostly. Was fine on its own. 3.6*

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

This Prosecco does the trick for us!  3.6✨

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

For the value, this is a great prosecco if you're looking for a ripe fruit expression. Hints of tropical fruit, honey and citrus make this a great wine for a warm summers night.

4.3 Delightful. 15.5% ABV. Opaque ruby in the glass. Notes of ripe red plumb and baked cherries, roasted coffee and bitter cocoa. Aged 3 years in barrel prior to bottling. Extraordinary.
This is a classic Amarone. Lots of chocolate and black cherry, mixed with oak and spices.  Lucious wine that goes down smooth and velvety. Cheers!
Deep in color, powder Coco. Give me a straw cause this is delicious.  Bigger in body but well balanced.  4th from the valpo tasting class.
Wonderful flavors: cherry chocolate earthy.  Light tannins, velvety on the tongue. Paired well with smoked meats and steak.
Beautiful amarone in Viinimessut  with friends. Full and round, smooth chocolate, vanilla,plum and mocha. The best I tasted during the fun evening.
Look.  Up in the sky.  It’s a bird.  It’s a plane.  It’s Superjam!  Nice dry taste.  Awesome nose.  Astringent and pleasantly complex finish
Excellent & Typical Amarone Classico from Masi winery, Veneto, Italy 🇮🇹. cherry chocolate plum oak raisin leather on the palate. Great Amarone wine 🍷!!
Dark ruby red, on nose sweet cherry and dark chocolate. On palate great balance, nice sweetness. Getting some nice aging hints. Drank in a magnum. Really enjoyed this
Stone, mushroom, fig, jammy red fruit, coffee, toffee, caramel, smoke. Full bodied with ripe fruit and loads of auxiliary flavors with an outstandingly long finish.
Superb Amarone. For me, the best. Let’s say in Isaan, to pair with spicy dishes made with “moo” or “ped”… explosive notes of red fruits and sweet chocolate…
Me oh my!!  I love amarone.   And umm, currant and cranberry and plum and leather and a bit of evergreen and tree bark on the nose. the tannins are beautiful I’m in love omg.
Objectively and sentimentally one of my favourite wines. Blackberry and toast on the nose. Palate has a unique and delightful blistered tomato. Exceptional mouth feel. Exquisitely balanced.
Bomb. Leathery, earthy (plant soil??) base for dark fruit nose, pop of dark cherry and a little cocoa before a long, bone dry, oaky finish. Great color, burnt brown through a candy-apple red

Very balanced - perfume intensity matches palate - both moderate and the raisin is not loud, and the acidity just right - again, all balanced, not memorable (sounds worse than I intend it), but hits the mark for the entire crowd (-a pleaser).

2015 this time. Usual dark red with legs when swirling. Fragrance has plumb, red berries, and vanilla. Taste adds rich oak and chocolate while keeping berries and some plumb. Tannins are smooth and keeps a fresh crispy acidity. Can be had with anything but cheese is excellent.

Decanted 60min. The colour is deep ruby and very transparent with good surface reflection lights. The aroma start with blackberry, red berry, prunes, spice and a touch of forest floor at the edge. The second layer the chocolate, herbs arising. The notes is complicate. Very intense and full on the on the palates with smooth tannins, firm structure with fine texture mouth feel. It is has balance acidic, homogenous and dry with finishes savory dried blackcurrant with little oak and touch of coffee.

Family winery since 1772 named after their 1st vineyard "Vaio dei Masi". Dark ruby red colored. Seductive & delicate bouquet with glacé cherry aromas and notes of ripe dark fruit, herbs, oak, tobacco & leather. Similar to the bouquet, the palate is powerful yet elegant, slowly revealing its complex flavors. Cherry, plum, dried fruit, cocoa, coffee & spices like vanilla & cinnamon merge with silky smooth tannins and soft mouthwatering acidity in a long dry finish with a sweet touch. Rightfully called 'Gentle Giant'!

Deep ruby in color with aromas of ripe red fruit, oak and forest floor. Medium dryness, medium tannins, medium high acidity, medium alcohol with flavours of black currant, cassis, fig, jam, leather, vanilla. Medium finish. Very good.

Intensive and little rough in nose, in mouth much better, sweet and juicy on the beginning, spicy and little tannic in a few seconds. Plum, vanilla, chocolate, oak, pepper. Long, smooth, pleasant spicy finish. Very good, expressive, complex Amarone.

A dirty, gorgeous wine! Through their process, they dry the pumice and then re-ferment it. A color of vintageruby. A beautiful aroma of musk, smoke, black tea, chili, cocoa, coffee, and dried dark fruit. A flavor blend of red fruit, light spice notes and cloves, dark chocolate cherries, and oak notes. Wonderfully balanced.   Vintage - 2017 Tasted in wine class

A delightful taste.  Cocoa nibs, dried figs and plums.  The nose is haunting and it brings a silky texture to the palate.  This wine will be a capable dinner guest for any red proteins.  Plenty of structure but the tannins are fine grained and well balanced.

Absolutely fantastic Amarone. Smooth and juicy, big and bold, fragrant and elegant. Powerful stuff. Plum, raisins, figs, leather, cherry, caramel. Got better glass on glass and by the end was simply majestic.

Leather, tobacco, and earthy notes on the nose. A beautiful velvety chocolate taste, very subtle on the fruit notes but not withheld too much. Slight notes of dark fruit and plum.  I could feel the stress leaving my body with each relaxing sip. Surprisingly smooth. One of my favorites.

Ripe black-skinned fruit (mostlycherry with some ripe plum), flowers, and baking with a whiff of cedar. Concentrated but still elegant with smooth, structured flavors. The first taste is  prune marinated in spirits, followed by black cherry, licorice and cocoa framed in velvety tannins. Really a great Amarone for the price.

4,3/5 Wonderful! Ripe red fruit dominating on the nose with hints of orange crunchy and taste of chocolate, little bit of raisins and again red berries! Well balanced and very enjoyable. Well recommended!

2017 4.5 pts  I have been coravining this vintage to find a perfect pairing with Desi food. Plum, dark chocolate, and high alcohol content define this Amarone. I found a perfect match on this Eid-ul-Fitre. Spicy flavors of kachcha gosht mutton biryani elevated this already perfect bottle. Spice and high alcohol content don't usually go well. This paring was a divine exception!

Really good. Grapes were dehydrated 105 days before ferment, giving it a robust body. Very spice-forward, with anise, baking spices, pepper, black fruits. Tannic, medium acidity. Heavier on alcohol, perfect with pairing with something robust and spicy (paired with Mexican-inspired dishes at Alinea, poached squash with mole, etc)
Lovely smooth Italian red. Subtle notes of dark fruits, cherry, strawberry, pepper. Nice warmth and enjoying it on this Christmas Day!

A great example of an amarone, with everything dialled up to 11. Huge intensity, body, acidity, tannins and alcohol. Notes of cooked red cherry, red and black plum, liquorice, vanilla, leather and meaty.

This was an amazing wine to end the evening with! Medium ruby colour, aromas of black licorice, mushroom, tobacco. Good acidity and tannins. After some breathing in the glass, you get a full body with a long finish. Flavours were complex and included plum, leather, and cherry. Very smooth. Looses bit of its expressiveness after some time in the glass. Time to drink the 2011 year?

This is a winner.  It started with raisins the moment we opened it and the smooth layered tastes validated its specialness.  We enjoyed the fruit, the hint of valpolicello earth, and the quality construction of this wine.  As a fan of Amarone , this is one of the better wines — especially when factoring the moderate price point.  Oca Bianca, Santa Margherita Ligure 2025.  🇮🇹🏆
Plum, blackcurrant, pepper, ruby, light tannic. Never expect so impressive. Mid complexity. But can't breath too long, the taste will become lighter. plum pepper black fruit

Incredibly easy to make it disappear! Restrained and well balanced. What a cracking wine for the price.  Black cherry, blackcurrant, barbecue meat aromas. Dry with flavours of vanilla, oak, leather, blackberry, raisin, pepper and a long finish.

Vintage 2021 - Opened 2025. Beautiful clean medium ruby colour. Nose is full of red fruit, cherry, red currant, slight blueberry, oak, vanilla. Finish is medium, with a slightly bitter after-taste, with dried fruit, plum, jammy. Very good red wine from Masi.

Our sommelier offered me to do the pairing myself describing the dish. The dish  had sauteed Skrei (codd), beetroot puree, shortbread (zwezerik) and parsley. On the list was this Soave with some exotic tones in the description. The original pairing was an unoaked Chardonnay. To me Soave usually is close to a Chard. Paired side by side the Chard was fresher/cleansing the palate. The Soave was more complementing the dish leveraging the beetroot - on its own a bit heavy on the flower tones.

WSET 3 The wine is a medium lemon color. The nose is of medium (+) intensity and has youthful aromas of peach, nectarine, lemon, chalk. Masi's classic white wine presents a fresh and balanced character. Cheers ! 🍇🥂

Intense aromas of ripe black cherries, with a herby edge of fresh sage.   This is Ripasso style - with a proportion of leftover skins from Amarone adding complexity in the form of leather, tobacco, and dark chocolate flavours.  This is beautifully structured with a medium+ body and a lovely balance of acidity and tannins. The tannins are surprisingly fine and lead to an elegant finish.  Paired well with the steak at a'Mare at the Crown in Sydney - Jan 2026.

RL = 3.8 & VV=4.0 🇮🇹🇮🇹🇮🇹 Corvina Veronese+Corvinone+ Rondinella+Sangiovesi (40/30/25/5)%  Alegrini's family is the leader of the Valpolicella Classic Style. The reasons are: more than 520 years dedicated to the vineyard development in it's more than 100 hectares and its different drying processes.  👁Dark Ruby w violet touch. 👃Tobacco & Raisins   💋Dried BlackBerry, black pepper, cherry, clove, vanilla w Subdued & smooth tannins  👚med+ 💫 Marked by its sweet fruit spectrum  ⏰Med.  permanence.
Very fruity with lot of Ripasso style. Red fresh berry and raisin plum raspberry vanilla and spices. Very good made and cheap.
An enduring favorite. So much of what is great about Amarone, but has what you want from a less soft Italian at the same time
oak cherry earthy blackberry allspice coriander  High alcohol, medium acid  Nice smooth finish.  Very enjoyable  Great qpr  Will buy again
Earthy with oak and tobacco notes. Bold with a dry finish. Lots of sediment on the last pour. Medium tannins and a deep ruby color.
Went great with the burger at One Flew South (ATL) nice red blend, slightly tart, blackberry, cherry, pretty smooth, medium body
Earthy and smoke hint of berry and brisk tannins. Pleasant and even better with food (rare for me personally). Delightful find
Sweet Strawberry, raisin, prune, light vanilla. Just a hint of sweetness from raisinated grapes. Medium+ acidity with a good finish.
Raisins, oak, balsamic and mushrooms. Interesting all the way with an old world aged feel. This will start a conversation! 2017
Ruby red with purple hues. Velvety texture with raisins, vanilla, black pepper, cloves. A chew of cigar. Great balance and complexity.
3.9 pts Taste nose : oak leather chocolate Color : purple bold Taste : almost sweet but not, a bit pepper landing, finish good tannic
Tart, and dry oakiness. Dusty and medium bodied, lowish tannins.  EDIT: opened up a bit the next day. Nice, rich cherry flavor, medium full body.
Med to high acidity with heavy legs.  Dried blackberries, kola, black pepper, black cherries with tannins being countered by the sugar. Makes for a decent bar offering.
Violet in the glass with a sweet aroma.  Cherry and plum on the palate with a nice peppery spice.  It was good with pasta and interesting on its own too
Medium tannins, licorice, grape tomatoes like tartness, some cherry and light tobacco, easy to drink and went really well with some Havarti cheese I had
2020 vintage Medium ruby color Cedar oak blackberry nose Dry oaky, plum, Medium-highhigh acidity, Medium-highhigh tannin finish Mariage with bulgogi
A full bodied Valpollicella classic. Plum and cherry explosion with cocoa and pepper on the end. Beautifully balanced expression of the corvina grape. This is a real winner!
Tasted at the CAL-ITAL Summer WineFest at the Golden Nugget Casino in Atlantic City, on Aug 29, 2015. A great wine festival presenting just a small pinch of California & Italian wines.
Nice deep purple Italian red. Musty blueberry blackberry nose. Still has all the caramel and raisin notes but they more muted. Medium plus body and acid. Solid tannins and an excellent wine!
I have to revise my previous opinion. This bottle is much better. Or I am much better.  Corvina coming through in spades. Finish remains medium long, but that dies not spoil the fun.
My wife and both love this wine. Concentrated but not overbearing, great with food or on its own. Lotta earth, almost barnyard, dark fruits and plum on the nose. One of the best under $20 wines IMO
Made of 100% Corvina (one of the main grapes in Amarone) from Venetian regional of Northern Italy.   Somewhat raisiny, some chocolate, earthy, with moderate body and tannins, very nice.
Huge wine, nice balanced tannins, and a great buy for the price! Red Fruit like Cherry, Raspberry, and raisin. Oak, Baking Spices, and Vanilla add texture and dynamics.

Unfortunately, I don’t have the vintage...why do Restaurants leave that off the menu?!?! But I’m drinking this at an Italian Restaurant inside Chicago’s O’Hare Airport. Smooth but dark and rich - baby Amarone fits the bill as it has all the hallmarks but is much more subtle and smooth.

Full body, high acid, high tannin, dry  Nose is blackberry, vanilla, cocoa  Palate is cherry, leather, cedar  Long finish, not the most complex, great food wine  Edit: changing this to a 4 after an hour of decanting. Becomes much more open with some nice spice on the nose. Well rounded. Great value at $20

Valpolicella always delivers. This Allegrini entry level wine was quite good. We were excited to find it on the French island of St Barths after a week of Bordeaux. Good aroma (gets better with time) of dried fruit, subtle raisin and butterscotch, and nice medium bodied wine.  Ideally paired with our Carbonara and Margherita pizza.  Wife rated this as 3.9…but I’m going with a 4.0.   La Casina, 2025, St Barths.

Best way to think of this is like a baby Amarone - 30% dried grapes and  70% fresh grapes.  A blend of 4 grapes from a single vineyard there’s lots of dried fruit - raspberry and cherry - and some vanilla.  The palate is much richer with more dark cherry, liquorice and cedar. It’s so smooth but you up want to pair this with food. Would go with a lot and went really well with risotto!  Bumped to 4 for the value for money!

Dry red wine made from a blend of grapes 70% Corvina, 25% Rondinella, 5% Sangiovese, grown in Valpolicella, in the Veneto region of Italy. Fermented using the ripasso method.  Aged in used French oak for 15 months.  Taste like, lovely rose petal aromas with dried black cherry, tobacco and grace notes of dried herbs and black pepper with a ripe, velvety texture. Very consistent and delicious, over delivers.

Super smooth. Light, fruity and very drinkable. I don't normally notice the aromas on pouring but the smell of cherry hit me instantly. Looks quite heavyweight on pouring yet light and fruity on the palette. Reviews mention all sorts of tastes going off but it's mainly cherry, vanilla and a hint of tobacco for me.   Overall very pleasant. What I call a "safe" wine. One to please many people's tastes. This won't be my last. Rating for a 2016.

A nose of cherry  smoke and oak make this eligible as a fantastic men's cologne, never mind a wine. Well balanced and potent, this is dry, fruity, and unmistakably Italian. Pair with strong cheese pasta dish for an authentic experience.

This punches above its weight class… $15 at Costco. Almost tastes like a baby amarone.. drank and while adding some to my tomato sauce for chicken parm. Great value and of course an excellent producer

Only had 1 glass in Vintage wine bar in Athens. Chosen by the sommelier who listened to my preferences and offered this. Light, fruity, smooth, very delicious! Paid €10 a glass so I'm glad to see it's highly rated

Complex and grippy, heavily blackcurrant scented on the nose and palate with raisin, balsamic, oak and chocolate coming in through the finish with green notes (?blackberry leaf ?), mouth puckering at first but eases into a rich and rewarding glass.

Hard to beat for the price. It has freshness and crunchy juicy fruit like cherry and pomegranate with some damp earthiness and vanilla blueberry muffins. Some pyrazines but very minor. Fine but grippy tannins.

Amazing with beef stew. So smooth, like velvet. Cherry, cola, chocolate, oak, vanilla, hint of soft ripe strawberry. Then there’s a lovely bitter orange note too. This is more prominent when without food. I love it. Bet it would age amazingly

Very nice surprise!! In overall as usual, it s the thing you expect the less that come with the most impressive surprises Very well balanced wine, nice acidity, lots of clove, dry fruits Will match many pasta dishes as well as meats

The nose opens with ripe red and black fruits, cherry and plum, alongside chocolate and black pepper. The palate mirrors those ripe fruits, enriched by chocolate, vanilla, coffee, subtle oak, black pepper and a touch of purple floral notes. The acidity is well balanced, with a gentle sweetness from the appassimento method adding depth. Good complexity, solid body.

Red ruby color, on the nose vanilla, black pepper and cloves and mostly raisined grapes !! On the palate it is velvety, with soft tannins low acidity and a long finish, one of the best wines I can find in Qatar.

Cherry oak on the nose, all dark fruit and a leather tone to it. A little earthy on the palatte. Great to pair with high protein foods. Age worthy, will improve with time. You have to try maybe you can buy again.   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *  Score Based on my New Point System Scale. (85-100 points)  Final Score - 90.5

A wine right on point for the money! Only 15.99 at total wine, this corvina grape is actually quite delicious! First time I bought it was 11/24. We opened it 2 days later on 11/26 to try along side a Sonoma Pinot Noir. This was definitely deeper and dark compares to the Pinot but it is still very much enjoyable! We liked it so much that we bought another bottle to enjoy on 11/28 with the honey ham for the day after Thanksgiving dinner.Notes of dark chocolate, earth, leather, dark berries, oak, tobacco, plum,&smoke

Smoky, garlic, on the nose truffle/cured meats, balanced with sweet and clear fruit like pomegranate and strawberry velvety and butter  No bite smooth finish easy to drink medium body  Sooo good with smoky food, black rice arancini at the Hilton

Earthy, woody aroma that's somewhat intense. A touch of stewed black fruits or raisin. Opens up on the palette with surprising acidity given the aroma. Really interesting, and tempting to drink the whole bottle
Deep flavor. High tannins but well balanced with a long finish. Not much in the nose. Not smokey. Sweet black fruits
Lighter in taste than it looks. Medium to dry but ends with a smooth finish. Hint of cherry. Great price. Consistently good wine.
Excellent. Wonderfully plummy nose. Berries, plums in the mouth. Light tannins. Silky looooong finish.
A delicious wine at a great value. Soft with notes of ripe fruit, oak, and a hint of spice. Ready to drink!
Really opened up since laste bottle, plum and exotic spice. Tannins really smoothed out with cherry and prune finish. Nice !
Deep ruby love. Boysenberry, blueberry and blackberry. Red currants. Tobacco. Mineral. Woody. Herbal. Dry. Acidic.
Great Italian blend. Goes perfect with homemade pizza tonight! I'm getting notes of dried cherries and blackberries
Cola-colored Italian intrigue. Good, quality red blend with bits of vanilla, oak, and earthy qualities.  Very good value and flavor!
Great balance in nose, leather, blackcurrant, pepper, oak, in palate long finish of drak fruits and burnt wood...excellent wine.
sweet berries , blackcurrant , leather , earthy , bouquet . taste of black pepper , mid to full bodies
Nose plums, oak and vanilla with a hint of smoke. Palate leans into more tart cherry. Not very tannic, but smooth and full bodied
Very soft and smooth on the palate. Earthy and mushroom nose. Nice tannins and dry fruits on the palate. Would buy again.
Excellent especially for price.  Complex with earthy tobacco notes, dark berry, spice, raisin and sweet tobacco.
cherry oak earthy
So enjoyable. Need to verbalize better soon!!! This was smooth and well rounded

Cherry, red fruit, licorice, nice acidity, fruity and jammy

Dark fruit, cocoa, cherry, raisin. Smooth, very nice.

Blackberry, oak, earthy and some light red fruit.  Soft tannins, medium bodied. 3.8/5

“Fruity but dry” says Chloe. 2 timers w Chlo and Deenz. Melanie says it’s great with steak.
